violations.text: Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Ice machine door covering located in the (inside ice machine) area was not in good repair. Materials shall be smooth, durable and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Observed the non-food contact surfaces of the (fans) have dust, and other debris located in 3-bay sink area of kitchen. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues/debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

The food prep sink in the front service area was lacking an adequate air gap. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

The floor and wall junctures under the three-bay sink and front hand sink and prep sink do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024
Ceiling tiles in the back service area are in poor repair (water damage observed). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2024
Observed various floor tiles missing underneath seafood display cases in front service area. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Inadequate lighting observed in walk-in cooler. Establishment shall replace lighting with brighter light bulb. The light intensity shall be at least 108 lux (10 foot candles) at 75 cm (30 inches) above the floor, in walk-in refrigeration units and dry FOOD storage areas and in other areas and rooms during periods of cleaning. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Several foods (see disposal form) were held past their seven day date marking. PIC voluntarily disposed of food at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Several dry ingredients packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC moved all unlabeled product behind service counter. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

The food prep sink in the front service area was lacking an adequate air gap. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

The floor and wall junctures under the three bay sink and front hand sink and prep sink do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024
Cieling tiles in the back service area are in poor repair (water damage observed). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2024
The date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023
